description: Records low-b equal-charge onset data and the corrected threshold rho(b). The old cubic-crossing tau(b) agrees through b=6 but fails at b=7.
threshold_formula: rho(b) = min{d >= 2b+1 : (b+1)*(d-1)^3 >= (b-1)*(d+1)^3}; tau(b) is retained only as the old cubic-crossing reference

provenance.13: defect 352 (sub-ceiling); matrix 10368x10976, min(s,t) 10368, rank 10016; see results/b5_decisive_result.json
provenance.14: defect 642 (sub-ceiling, last depth below tau); matrix 13182x13500, min(s,t) 13182, rank 12540; two primes 100003/100019; raw logs logs/b5_d14_p*.txt
provenance.15: defect 960; matrix 16464x16384, min(s,t) 16384, rank 15424; two-prime consistent; see results/b5_decisive_result.json; reproduce: --bd 5 15 100003 and --bd 5 15 100019
provenance.16: defect 960 (stored prior plateau check, two primes; NOT in the raw-log set); matrix 20250x19652, min(s,t) 19652, rank 18692; recompute: --bd 5 16 100003 and --bd 5 16 100019 on a >4 GB host

refuted_bounds.a_i>=2b: In the computed/proven cases through b=5, this bound is sub-ceiling (b=3: d=6 -> 4 vs 192; b=5: d=10 -> 6 vs 960). This is not a proof for all b.
refuted_bounds.a_i>=2b+1: In the computed/proven cases through b=5, this bound is sub-ceiling (b=3: d=7 -> 32 vs 192; b=5: d=11 -> 48 vs 960). This is not a proof for all b.

cubic_reference: reference value = (b+1) * prod_i (a_i - (2b-1)); the ceiling threshold is determined by the source-target condition E >= F, not by this reference in general
reproduce:
python3 scripts/saturation_threshold.py        (full table, b=2,3 fresh + b=4 d=11)
python3 scripts/saturation_threshold.py --gate (fast onset assertion used by reproduce.py)
python3 scripts/saturation_threshold.py --bd B D [PRIME]  (defect at one charge; e.g. --bd 5 15 -> 960, --bd 5 16 -> 960; heavier, optional second prime)
python3 scripts/run_decisive_flint.py          (b=4 decisive sweep, depths 11/12/13 x three primes -> 480)
